nodes.description: Cardiac valve disease belongs to the extra-skeletal connective-tissue involvement of Morquio syndrome and is well described in both subtypes.

evidence.reference_title: International guidelines for the management and treatment of Morquio A syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Morquio A syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IVA) is a lysosomal storage disorder associated with skeletal and joint abnormalities and significant non-skeletal manifestations including respiratory disease, spinal cord compression, cardiac disease, impaired vision, hearing loss, and dental problems.

evidence.reference_title: Morquio B disease: From pathophysiology towards diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: It is characterised by dysostosis multiplex, ligament laxity, mildly coarse facies and heart valve defects due to keratan sulphate accumulation, predominantly in the cartilage.

nodes.description: Tracheal reconstructive procedures or tracheotomy may be required for life-threatening airway obstruction in severe disease.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: Tracheostomy has often been required for tracheal obstruction

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: three patients with severe tracheal obstruction and respiratory distress showed improvement in their symptoms after undergoing successful tracheal reconstructive surgery

evidence.reference_title: Novel approach for tracheal resection in Morquio a syndrome with end-stage critical airway obstruction: a UK case series
evidence.snippet: Our findings demonstrate the effectiveness of the transcervical approach with partial manubriectomy for improving respiratory function and quality of life for individuals with advanced airway obstruction.

nodes.description: Glycosaminoglycan storage in cartilage, ligaments, and their extracellular matrix balloons chondrocytes and impedes endochondral ossification and chondrogenesis.
nodes.id: Cartilage matrix dysfunction and impaired endochondral ossification

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: As a result, partially degraded GAGs accumulate in bone, ligaments, and cartilage, as well as the extracellular matrix (ECM) of these tissues, impeding endochondral ossification and chondrogenesis

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: Examination of the tissues demonstrated systemic storage materials in multiple tissues, as well as severely ballooned and vacuolated chondrocytes in the trachea, humerus, knee cartilage, and lung bronchus.

nodes.description: Surgical stabilization of the craniovertebral junction is used when odontoid hypoplasia and atlantoaxial instability threaten the spinal cord.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: Therefore, most patients require several surgeries to alleviate severe orthopedic complications, such as cervical spinal decompression and fusion, limb osteotomy, hemiepiphysiodesis, and hip reconstruction/replacement

nodes.description: Craniovertebral instability and cervical canal compromise can compress the cervical spinal cord and produce myelopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with MPS IVA, particularly with the severe form, often do not survive past their twenties, as the most attributed causes of mortality and morbidity are spinal cord compression, instability of the C1-C2 joint, and airway compromise including tracheal obstruction

evidence.reference_title: Morquio-B disease: Clinical and genetic characteristics of a distinct GLB1-related dysostosis multiplex.
evidence.snippet: Corneal clouding, cardiac valve pathology, hepatosplenomegaly, spinal cord compression were infrequent

nodes.description: Storage outside cartilage affects cornea and cardiac valves, contributing to corneal clouding, impaired vision, hearing loss, and valve disease.
nodes.id: Corneal and valvular connective tissue involvement

evidence.reference_title: International guidelines for the management and treatment of Morquio A syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Morquio A syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IVA) is a lysosomal storage disorder associated with skeletal and joint abnormalities and significant non-skeletal manifestations including respiratory disease, spinal cord compression, cardiac disease, impaired vision, hearing loss, and dental problems.

evidence.reference_title: Morquio B disease: From pathophysiology towards diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: It is characterised by dysostosis multiplex, ligament laxity, mildly coarse facies and heart valve defects due to keratan sulphate accumulation, predominantly in the cartilage.

nodes.description: Corneal clouding is a characteristic extra-skeletal manifestation of Morquio syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mucopolysaccharidosis type IVA (Morquio A): a close differential diagnosis of spondylo-epiphyseal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: MPS type IVA (Morquio A syndrome) has predominant musculoskeletal system involvement and corneal clouding with normal intelligence and can be misdiagnosed as primary skeletal disorders in clinical practice.

evidence.reference_title: Cataracts in Morquio syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IV A).
evidence.snippet: In addition to the characteristic dwarfism with skeletal deformities, odontoid anomalies, hearing loss and corneal clouding, the authors found almost identical lens opacities in all three patients.

nodes.description: Odontoid hypoplasia, atlantoaxial instability, and cervical canal narrowing destabilize the craniovertebral junction.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with MPS IVA, particularly with the severe form, often do not survive past their twenties, as the most attributed causes of mortality and morbidity are spinal cord compression, instability of the C1-C2 joint, and airway compromise including tracheal obstruction

nodes.description: Morquio syndrome produces a progressive dysostosis multiplex pattern with vertebral, thoracic, and appendicular skeletal deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Morquio B disease: From pathophysiology towards diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: It is characterised by dysostosis multiplex, ligament laxity, mildly coarse facies and heart valve defects due to keratan sulphate accumulation, predominantly in the cartilage.

evidence.reference_title: Chondroitin sulfate disaccharide is a specific and sensitive biomarker for mucopolysaccharidosis type IVA.
evidence.snippet: Mucopolysaccharidosis type IVA (MPS IVA) is an inborn error of glycosaminoglycan (GAG) catabolism characterized by a deficiency of the lysosomal enzyme, N-acetylgalactosamine 6-sulphatase (GALNS).

evidence.reference_title: GM1 gangliosidosis and Morquio B disease: an update on genetic alterations and clinical findings.
evidence.snippet: GM1 gangliosidosis and Morquio B syndrome, both arising from beta-galactosidase (GLB1) deficiency, are very rare lysosomal storage diseases

evidence.reference_title: Morquio B disease: From pathophysiology towards diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: Morquio B disease is an attenuated phenotype within the spectrum of beta galactosidase (GLB1) deficiencies.

nodes.description: Valgus deformity of the knees is a common lower-extremity consequence of Morquio skeletal dysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: The accumulation of these GAGs leads to distinguishing features as skeletal dysplasia with disproportionate dwarfism, short neck, kyphoscoliosis, pectus carinatum, tracheal obstruction, coxa valga, genu valgum, and joint laxity.

nodes.description: Hearing loss is a recurrent Morquio manifestation that travels with the extra-skeletal connective-tissue disease burden.

evidence.reference_title: International guidelines for the management and treatment of Morquio A syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Morquio A syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IVA) is a lysosomal storage disorder associated with skeletal and joint abnormalities and significant non-skeletal manifestations including respiratory disease, spinal cord compression, cardiac disease, impaired vision, hearing loss, and dental problems.

evidence.reference_title: Cataracts in Morquio syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IV A).
evidence.snippet: In addition to the characteristic dwarfism with skeletal deformities, odontoid anomalies, hearing loss and corneal clouding, the authors found almost identical lens opacities in all three patients.

nodes.description: Odontoid hypoplasia contributes to craniovertebral instability and risk of cervical cord compromise.

evidence.reference_title: Morquio-B disease: Clinical and genetic characteristics of a distinct GLB1-related dysostosis multiplex.
evidence.snippet: Forty-one of 51 cases with informative clinical data had pure MBD including progressive growth impairment, kyphoscoliosis, coxa/genua valga, joint laxity, platyspondyly, odontoid hypoplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Cataracts in Morquio syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IV A).
evidence.snippet: In addition to the characteristic dwarfism with skeletal deformities, odontoid anomalies, hearing loss and corneal clouding, the authors found almost identical lens opacities in all three patients.

nodes.description: Joint laxity distinguishes Morquio syndrome from many other mucopolysaccharidoses, which more often cause joint stiffness.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: The accumulation of these GAGs leads to distinguishing features as skeletal dysplasia with disproportionate dwarfism, short neck, kyphoscoliosis, pectus carinatum, tracheal obstruction, coxa valga, genu valgum, and joint laxity.

evidence.reference_title: Morquio-B disease: Clinical and genetic characteristics of a distinct GLB1-related dysostosis multiplex.
evidence.snippet: Forty-one of 51 cases with informative clinical data had pure MBD including progressive growth impairment, kyphoscoliosis, coxa/genua valga, joint laxity, platyspondyly, odontoid hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Keratan sulfate accumulates in both Morquio subtypes; Type A also stores chondroitin-6-sulfate. Storage is concentrated in cartilage and other connective tissues, where distended lysosomes initiate downstream tissue injury.
nodes.id: Keratan sulfate-dominant lysosomal storage in cartilage and connective tissue

evidence.reference_title: Chondroitin sulfate disaccharide is a specific and sensitive biomarker for mucopolysaccharidosis type IVA.
evidence.snippet: Consequently, partially degraded GAG, chondroitin 6-sulfate (CS) and keratan sulfate (KS), accumulate in the lysosomes of affected cells, primarily in cartilage resulting in skeletal disease.

evidence.reference_title: Imbalanced substrate specificity of mutant beta-galactosidase in patients with Morquio B disease.
evidence.snippet: These findings suggest that imbalanced substrate specificity of the mutant beta-Gals induces predominant accumulation of keratan sulfate and a rationale for performing differential diagnostic analysis for both disorders.

nodes.description: Chest wall deformity is a common skeletal manifestation of Morquio syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: The accumulation of these GAGs leads to distinguishing features as skeletal dysplasia with disproportionate dwarfism, short neck, kyphoscoliosis, pectus carinatum, tracheal obstruction, coxa valga, genu valgum, and joint laxity.

nodes.description: Rehabilitation, pain control, and mobility aids help preserve function and reduce disability from chronic skeletal disease, even though they do not correct the underlying lysosomal defect.

evidence.reference_title: International guidelines for the management and treatment of Morquio A syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Physical therapy and pain medication can be beneficial to manage musculoskeletal manifestations in some patients.

nodes.description: Morquio syndrome expresses its core phenotype as progressive dysostosis multiplex, short stature, kyphoscoliosis, limb deformity, and joint laxity.
nodes.id: Progressive skeletal dysplasia and joint laxity

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: The accumulation of these GAGs leads to distinguishing features as skeletal dysplasia with disproportionate dwarfism, short neck, kyphoscoliosis, pectus carinatum, tracheal obstruction, coxa valga, genu valgum, and joint laxity.

evidence.reference_title: Morquio-B disease: Clinical and genetic characteristics of a distinct GLB1-related dysostosis multiplex.
evidence.snippet: Forty-one of 51 cases with informative clinical data had pure MBD including progressive growth impairment, kyphoscoliosis, coxa/genua valga, joint laxity, platyspondyly, odontoid hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Progressive upper-airway and tracheal narrowing reduce ventilation and can lead to respiratory distress, chronic respiratory disease, and mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with MPS IVA, particularly with the severe form, often do not survive past their twenties, as the most attributed causes of mortality and morbidity are spinal cord compression, instability of the C1-C2 joint, and airway compromise including tracheal obstruction

evidence.snippet: Morquio A syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IVA) is a lysosomal storage disorder associated with skeletal and joint abnormalities and significant non-skeletal manifestations including respiratory disease, spinal cord compression, cardiac disease, impaired vision, hearing loss, and dental problems.

nodes.description: Disproportionate short stature results from impaired endochondral bone growth and progressive skeletal dysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: The accumulation of these GAGs leads to distinguishing features as skeletal dysplasia with disproportionate dwarfism, short neck, kyphoscoliosis, pectus carinatum, tracheal obstruction, coxa valga, genu valgum, and joint laxity.

nodes.description: Cervical instability and canal compromise can produce clinically significant spinal cord compression and myelopathy, especially in severe Type A disease.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.

nodes.description: Glycosaminoglycan-loaded tracheal cartilage together with thoracic and cervicothoracic skeletal distortion produces fixed upper-airway and tracheal narrowing.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: This autopsied case with MPS IVA addresses the importance of tracheal obstruction for morbidity and mortality of the disease

evidence.reference_title: Novel approach for tracheal resection in Morquio a syndrome with end-stage critical airway obstruction: a UK case series
evidence.snippet: These manifestations of end-stage disease are engendered by an insidious progression of multi-level airway pathologies, comprising of tracheomalacia, stenosis, tortuosity and 'buckling'.

nodes.description: In Morquio type A, GALNS deficiency blocks lysosomal degradation of keratan sulfate and chondroitin-6-sulfate.

evidence.reference_title: Chondroitin sulfate disaccharide is a specific and sensitive biomarker for mucopolysaccharidosis type IVA.
evidence.snippet: Mucopolysaccharidosis type IVA (MPS IVA) is an inborn error of glycosaminoglycan (GAG) catabolism characterized by a deficiency of the lysosomal enzyme, N-acetylgalactosamine 6-sulphatase (GALNS).

nodes.description: Weekly intravenous recombinant GALNS replaces the deficient Type A enzyme, lowers storage biomarkers, and improves endurance, respiratory function, and growth-related outcomes. Skeletal, tracheal, and ocular tissues remain only partially responsive because of limited penetration.
nodes.id: Type A elosulfase alfa enzyme replacement therapy

evidence.reference_title: Diagnostic journey and impact of enzyme replacement therapy for mucopolysaccharidosis IVA: a sibling control study.
evidence.snippet: Early recognition, diagnosis, and treatment of this progressive, multisystem disease by enzyme replacement therapy (ERT) can lead to improved outcomes and reduced mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnostic journey and impact of enzyme replacement therapy for mucopolysaccharidosis IVA: a sibling control study.
evidence.snippet: All three patients demonstrated improvements in growth, 6-min walk distance, joint range of motion, and respiratory function after 30 months of ERT.

evidence.reference_title: Safety and clinical activity of elosulfase alfa in pediatric patients with Morquio A syndrome (mucopolysaccharidosis IVA) less than 5 y.
evidence.snippet: Early intervention with elosulfase alfa is well-tolerated and produces a decrease in uKS and a trend toward improvement in growth.

evidence.reference_title: Enzyme replacement therapy: efficacy and limitations.
evidence.snippet: While ERT is effective in reducing urinary glycosaminoglycans (GAGs) and liver and spleen volume, cartilaginous organs such as the trachea and bronchi, bones and eyes are poorly impacted by ERT probably due to limited penetration in the specific tissue.

nodes.description: In Morquio type B, GLB1 mutations create a beta-galactosidase deficiency phenotype in which keratan sulfate handling is disproportionately impaired, producing a skeletal/connective-tissue presentation rather than classic GM1 gangliosidosis.
nodes.id: Type B GLB1 deficiency with keratan sulfate-biased catalytic loss

evidence.reference_title: GM1 gangliosidosis and Morquio B disease: an update on genetic alterations and clinical findings.
evidence.snippet: GM1 gangliosidosis and Morquio B syndrome, both arising from beta-galactosidase (GLB1) deficiency, are very rare lysosomal storage diseases

evidence.reference_title: Imbalanced substrate specificity of mutant beta-galactosidase in patients with Morquio B disease.
evidence.snippet: These findings suggest that imbalanced substrate specificity of the mutant beta-Gals induces predominant accumulation of keratan sulfate and a rationale for performing differential diagnostic analysis for both disorders.

nodes.description: Tracheal and upper-airway narrowing contribute to progressive respiratory compromise and procedure-related airway risk.

evidence.reference_title: Natural history of Morquio A patient with tracheal obstruction from birth to death.
evidence.snippet: This autopsied case with MPS IVA addresses the importance of tracheal obstruction for morbidity and mortality of the disease
